general_info.definition: Graphene Oxide Quantum Dots (GOQDs) are nano-sized, oxygen-functionalized graphene derivatives that exhibit tunable photoluminescence and strong third-order optical nonlinearities

optical.excitation_max: 250 nm and 450 nm (for two distinct emission bands)
optical.fluorescence_lifetime: 1.6 ns (for 513 nm emission) and 0.6 ns (for 540 nm emission)

applications.performance: One-photon figure of merit (W) = 4.4-7.8, two-photon figure of merit (T) < 0.2 over 720-920 nm range, satisfying W > 1 and T < 1 requirements for optical switching devices. The GOQDs also show strong nonlinear refraction and weak nonlinear absorption in the wavelength range from 720nm to 920nm.
applications.comparative_advantage: could be used for low-cost all-optical switching devices in the near infrared region. Strong nonlinear refraction and weak nonlinear absorption in the wavelength range from 720nm to 920nm.
